title: Sandvik wins order for Glencore’s Alumbrera mine
Gold Slips as Stalled U.S.-Iran Talks Push Oil Higher
body: LIVE Last Updated: 1 hour ago By Giulia Petroni, Dow Jones Newswires Gold futures slipped as stalled U.S.-Iran negotiations and rising oil prices keep inflation concerns elevated. "The continued blockage of a key route for roughly one-fifth of global oil flows has sustained the energy shock, reinforcing expectations that central banks may keep interest rates higher for longer, a major headwind for non-yielding bullion," said Soojin Kim from MUFG. "Investors are also closely watching potential leadership changes at the Fed for clues on future monetary policy."

title: CIBC Lifts PT on Hudbay Minerals Inc. (HBM) As Part of Fiscal Q1 Preview
body: Oops, something went wrong Hudbay Minerals Inc. (NYSE: HBM ) is one of the best copper stocks to invest in now . CIBC lifted the price target on Hudbay Minerals Inc. (NYSE:HBM) to C$46 from C$42 on April 21, reiterating an Outperform rating on the shares. The firm adjusted price targets in the gold and base metals group as part of a fiscal Q1 preview, stating that it believes the 20% selloff in gold from its January high and the “flip-flop” in Federal Reserve funds expectations will “support a bounce in the asset’s price.” CIBC remains optimistic on gold and sees a solid entry point at current share levels. title: Is Barrick Mining Corporation (B) One of the Best Copper Stocks to Invest in Now?
body: Oops, something went wrong Barrick Mining Corporation (NYSE: B ) is one of the best copper stocks to invest in now . On April 21, CIBC cut the price target on Barrick Mining Corporation (NYSE:B) to $63 from $67 while reaffirming an Outperform rating on the shares. The firm adjusted price targets for the companies under its coverage in its gold and base metals group as part of a fiscal Q1 preview, and stated that it believes the 20% selloff in gold from its January high and the “flip-flop” in Federal Reserve funds expectations will “support a bounce in the asset’s price.” The firm further expressed optimism on gold, saying that it sees a solid entry point at current share levels.
